title: leucanthemopsis minima
intro: Leucanthemopsis minima, also known as Alpine Daisy, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Leucanthemopsis minima include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially lowering blood pressure. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to address a range of health issues.
